programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A Navy reservist is found dead in his home with marks around his neck, and Gibbs and the team discover he was working on a highly classified case for the department of defence. Guest starring Jamie Lee Curtis

programme.desc: A petty officer is killed by a stolen surveillance drone and the team links it to elusive terrorist Benham Parsa. programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Sharon and Tracey have a new sideline in double-glazing, and a new, ruthless saleswoman in Dorien. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke and Linda Robson

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Sam, Diane and Nikki see a young woman fall from a walkway on the Cole Lane estate and learn that the woman's alcoholic husband holds himself responsible for her injuries. Meanwhile, Mickey sets up an underground operation to investigate the activities of a pub card-sharp. Pooja Shah guest stars

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Donna finds her father drunk ahead of his operation, but Ric quickly sees through her efforts to cover for him and cancels the surgery. Connie hopes to impress the hospital chairman, but knows having Jac in theatre will hinder her chances, and Daisha stands up for Oliver when Linden accuses him of being the ward joker, even though he has proved himself capable of doing the job

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Tess treats a woman who has been severely beaten, and attempts to find out how she came by her injuries, while Simon and Lara celebrate their stag and hen nights, but end up at the same venue. It soon transpires that the bride-to-be isn't going to let Simon out of her sight. Bex tries to get back together with Luke, but he ends up kissing Claire

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: A murder hunt is launched when Reshna's body is pulled out of the Thames and evidence emerges that she was having an affair with a fellow student. Meanwhile, Diane and Mickey are left in the dark when an eccentric mugging victim claims to be an undercover officer and Scotland Yard refuse to confirm or deny her story. Jenny Eclair stars

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Alan surprises Carol with a suggestion about their future, while David mulls over his own past with Carol. Gita is shocked when Guppy turns up at her flat with Meena

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: It's Carol and Alan's anniversary. Ted plans an opening party for the new shop and Ian asks David to walk Cindy home

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Having finally been convinced of Regina's duplicity, George warns Elizabeth to be careful of giving the game away. James takes Harry to a private party in Sydney, but Henry is furious. Roy continues to act as Sarah's protector and is on hand when Gordon Walsh comes calling

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Dr Turner and Shelagh are at the forefront of the advances in medical technology, and begin a campaign to ensure better respiratory health for their patients. However, Timothy fears their own lifestyle is not good for them and tries to encourage his parents to give up smoking. Violet injures her back and is forced to leave the shop in Fred's hands, a decision that strengthens their relationship but which proves to have other, unforeseen consequences. A new mother's past comes to light, with devastating repercussions for her family. Nurse Crane draws on her own experiences to help her

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Poplar is rocked by a series of violent attacks on women, and a prostitute is too ashamed to report what she knows - but her actions lead to a dangerous man roaming the streets, with devastating consequences for the residents of Nonnatus House. A mother tries to keep her daughter's pregnancy a secret to avoid gossip, putting her life in danger. The Turners go on a long overdue holiday, but the weather ruins their plans

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: The introduction of the contraceptive pill is welcomed by Dr Turner, but some of his colleagues are less keen on the idea. Patsy tries to help a bargee who wants to give birth among her own community rather than in a maternity ward. Nurse Crane deals with an emergency during a storm, and a familiar face returns to Nonnatus House - but has been changed by her time away

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Lenny hears a voice from above, and decides he must wait patiently for more information, leaving the gang to make sure he is well-equipped to receive messages. Comedian Bobby Ball returns as the gullible pickle factory worker, with Peter Sallis and Frank Thornton

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Canon Fox announces that the Duke of Frome will be staying with Lady Felicia at Montague and attending a full Christmas Mass at St Mary's. Father Brown and Mrs McCarthy set about making the arrangements, but when the donkey goes missing, laryngitis hits the choir and a tramp is found sleeping in the confessional box, it looks like Fox's plans are set to be thwarted. Lady Felicia welcomes her cousin the Duke, his wife and baby to the house, but later a nursemaid discovers that the infant is missing from his cot beside a heavily sleeping Nanny Langton. Mark Williams stars

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: A scientist studying a live volcano is found dead halfway up the mountain, and the evidence points to a heart attack. Humphrey is convinced one of the dead man's colleagues murdered him - an unlikely possibility, since they all have alibis. Away from the investigation, the inspector eagerly awaits Martha's return to the island, while JP settles into married life and Dwayne makes an error of judgment while talking to a journalist. Kris Marshall, Josephine Jobert and Danny John-Jules star in the sun-soaked whodunit, with guest appearances from Douglas Hodge, Natasha Little and Sally Bretton

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: A police informant resurfaces after 17 years on the run, and claims to have details implicating a crook-turned-celebrity in an unsolved armed robbery. Sandra accompanies Strickland to the Hero of the Year Awards, where the recipient of the title is an old rival of Gerry's - who is brought in to work on the case. With guest star Steven Berkoff

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: Three highly skilled Chinese agents arrive in London, so the team sets out to discover the purpose of their visit. Ruth goes undercover at a major research corporation, where she learns the trio are looking for a scientist who has discovered a new formula worth billions, so Beth enlists a mole to infiltrate the Chinese security services. However, his trustworthiness is soon called into question. Colin Salmon guest stars, with Nicola Walker and Sophia Myles

programme.title: Catherine Cookson's The Round Tower
programme.desc: Feature-length drama set in the 1950s, starring Emilia Fox as the rebellious teenage daughter of a successful businessman and his devoted wife, whose disenchantment with her privileged upbringing ultimately provokes a scandal in their small town. Keith Barron and Jan Harvey also star

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Sharon and Tracey receive marital advice from Dorien as they prepare to welcome their husbands home from prison, but it is not long before they find themselves making excuses for her to Marcus. Starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

programme.desc: Elliot's doubts get the better of him and he withdraws from Tara's team on the morning of the high-profile surgery. Ric informs Donna there is nothing more he can do for her father, so she buries herself in work to avoid facing the truth about his condition, and Oliver worries he is only getting good marks because of his friendship with Chrissie - but a misdirected e-mail jeopardises that. With guest star Kellie Bright (Rock & Chips)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Lara's big day is put in jeopardy when Abs makes a shocking discovery, and the plane taking Simon to the wedding crashes and explodes after Jim loses control. Oblivious to what has happened, the bride instructs Harry to take her to the airport, and phones ahead to buy a one-way ticket to Australia, while a distraught Bex clashes with Claire and a fist-fight breaks out

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Emma is feeling raw after her marital difficulties boiled over into violence and shows her tough side to new recruit Beth Green (former EastEnder Louisa Lytton) as they investigate a woman's claim her baby has been stolen. Jack is anxious to prove he's a worthy DCI and agrees to go undercover with Sam to get a lead on the illegal baby-selling business, but his mistake puts the DI in danger

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Grant helps Phil avoid the childbirth classes. Meena tells Gita she is marrying Guppy. Meanwhile, Ted is angry after Sarah has a makeover

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Cindy gets drunk at a dinner party for Pat and David. Sarah steals CDs to give to Tiffany to thank her. Sanjay and Guppy agree to a truce and Rob still can't go fishing

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Sarah is warned by Carolyn that Regina has escaped her intended incarceration in an asylum and to be on guard, but Sarah believes her to be long gone. Elizabeth is exasperated with Rose who refuses to divulge her mistresses whereabouts

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Dr Turner is horrified to realise he may be partly to blame as the cause of the recent rise in birth deformities comes to light, and tries to prevent further cases. Sister Evangelina returns to work, but has clearly been affected badly by her stroke, and her health continues to deteriorate. A pregnant bride goes into labour during her wedding reception, while her mother is forced to come to terms with her grandchild being conceived out of wedlock

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Christmas special of the period drama, which catches up with the nurses and midwives of Nonnatus House in 1962. They receive a letter from a doctor running a mission clinic on the Eastern Cape of South Africa, who is in desperate need of their help to save her urgently needed hospital from closure. The nurses head to Africa to lend a hand, finding the clinic understaffed, underfunded and with a poor water supply, but overwhelmed with patients who need their help more than anyone ever has before. Sinead Cusack guest stars

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: The nuns and midwives return home after their trip to South Africa, to find Nonnatus House has come under new management. The formidable Sister Ursula (Harriet Walter) has taken charge and introduced some drastic changes to the way the clinic is run, which Sister Julienne, bound by her vow of obedience, is powerless to oppose. The midwives come to the aid of a victim of domestic violence whose husband is about to be released from prison, and who risks losing everything if she seeks a divorce. Sister Mary Cynthia suffers further anxiety attacks, and Patsy receives distressing news about her father

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Clegg takes care of a friendly dog for the day, but when Truly, Billy and Alvin visit, the canine ferociously guards his new minder's house, leaving the gang to spend a day on the doorstep thinking up ambitious plans to negotiate their entry. Comedy, starring Peter Sallis and Frank Thornton

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The priest is asked to look after Lady Felicia's wayward niece Bunty, who soon finds herself suspected of murder. Starring Mark Williams, with Emer Kenny

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The Saint Marie Literary Festival is rocked by the death of a young literature student - and Florence's childhood friend - whose body is discovered at the bottom of a cliff. Suicide seems the likely cause, but as ever Humphrey isn't so keen to jump to conclusions - even with the presence of a note in the victim's bag. But with everyone the dead girl knew at the festival, who could have pushed her over the edge? Away from the investigation, the inspector is busy preparing the shack for Martha's stay. Sun-soaked whodunit, guest starring Fiona Allen, Ramon Tikaram and Monica Dolan

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The squad investigates a long-running feud between two family ice-cream businesses, which resulted in a shooting and several armed raids 10 years ago. Standing enlists the aid of his daughter in cracking the case, and she discovers evidence of drug dealing - but Pullman does not approve of her involvement. With Dennis Waterman and Amanda Redman, alongside guest star Jodie Kelly

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: In a move to bring peace to Palestine and Israel, the US president travels to London to chair secret talks between negotiators from both countries, but the threat from a journalist to print the story indicates there is a leak. As Lucas investigates, he discovers a plan to assassinate the president, putting the team under immense pressure to locate the sniper and prevent an international scandal. Starring Richard Armitage

programme.title: Catherine Cookson's Colour Blind
programme.desc: Drama from the best-selling author. Set between the wars, it concerns a girl who returns to her Tyneside home newly married to her black husband, only to encounter bigotry from all sides, to say nothing of her obsessed brother's animosity. Niamh Cusack, Tony Armatrading and Dearbhla Molloy star

programme.desc: Tracey's dream of having Darryl back home from prison finally comes true - but it may turn out to be a nightmare. Comedy, starring Linda Robson

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Tracey and Darryl's efforts to make up for lost time are continuously thwarted by Sharon and Garth, while Dorien's marital strife seems set to cause further upheaval for Chigwell's ne'er-do-well clan. Comedy, with Pauline Quirke, Lesley Joseph and Linda Robson

Chrissie tends Oliver's wounds after he is assaulted. Tony Britton guest stars

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Part one of two. Abs is consumed by guilt as news of Simon's death begins to spread. Two children go missing after visiting a relative at the hospital, and suspicion soon falls on a local convicted paedophile. When one of the youngsters is found trapped in an underground drain, Nina ignores Luke's pleas to wait for the fire brigade. Former EastEnder Lucy Benjamin guest stars

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Will and Nikki investigate the suspicious death of a child protection officer on the Cheetham estate. New recruit Beth delivers her first caution following an affray between a car salesman and a woman protesting against the dangers of 4x4 vehicles, but there's more to the conflict than first meets the eye. Boothby Graffoe guest stars

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Phil's still on edge about Frank being back in the Square. Gita plans a surprise for Sanjay and Guppy. Sarah goes for an interview at Walford Comprehensive

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Ricky is overjoyed by Frank's news, but it's gloves off for Sam and Bianca

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: The death of Sharon and Tracey's Aunt Sylvie soon has them jetting off to Hollywood in search of their real father. Festive special of the comedy from 1993, starring Pauline Quirke and Linda Robson, with guest appearances by George Hamilton, George Wendt and Richard Branson

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Sister Ursula insists the midwives do not become emotionally involved with their patients, but her harsh new approach proves hard to cope with in the face of two devastating cases. A father-to-be is badly injured in an explosion, leaving him unable to work to support his family, and a couple with dwarfism expecting their first child are warned that their child may be stillborn, a situation that leaves Patsy confronted with one of the hardest decisions of her life

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: The government plans to close down small hospitals in favour of larger establishments. Dr Turner and Shelagh prepare for an inspection that will decide the fate of Nonnatus House - but the staff is shocked to find Sister Ursula has no intention of helping to save the maternity home. Barbara comes to the aid of a woman from the Chinese community who is struggling to get on with her mother-in-law, and whose baby is seriously ill

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: A woman expecting her third child realises she cannot afford to care for her baby, so makes the painful decision to give it away to her wealthy but childless cousin. Shelagh befriends the woman in the next hospital bed and Dr Turner confides in Timothy about his worst fears. Sister Julienne looks for a new recruit to join the midwives, and the community prepares to celebrate the opening of a new park

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: After concluding she needs a new means of transport, Auntie Wainwright sends Tom and Smiler on a mission to collect her new vehicle - but it doesn't quite live up to their expectations. Jean Alexander, Tom Owen and Stephen Lewis star

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The priest struggles to promote understanding when a group of pagans are suspected of killing one of their own. Crime drama, starring Mark Williams

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Humphrey and Martha's romantic weekend on a remote island is cut short when the hotel owner is stabbed to death in his room in what appears to be an attempted robbery. But since the flower beds outside the window are undisturbed, Humphrey is certain that one of the victim's associates played a part in his death. There's just one problem - since the DI had a direct view of the only staircase leading up to the room, he is the alibi for all the suspects. Meanwhile, JP worries about his marriage and seeks relationship advice from Dwayne

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: A man wrongly convicted of burning down a school is released, and seeks Halford's help in tracking down the culprit. Standing meets a woman who claims to be his daughter (played by Dennis Waterman's real-life daughter Hannah) but Lane is not convinced she is telling the truth. Pullman is offered a promotion to head of the Murder Squad. Kevin Whately and Joe Absalom guest star

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: It is agreed that Section D will use American security system Cybershell to avoid security breaches, and Lucas is assigned to protect White House cryptographer Daniella Ortiz as she installs the necessary codes. However, Tariq discovers computers at the Grid have been infiltrated by suspected Russian and Chinese hackers, and it is only a matter of time before they will have access to all the information held by MI5 and the CIA

programme.title: Catherine Cookson's Tilly Trotter
programme.desc: Part one of two. Period drama about a 19-year-old girl who is attacked after a party celebrating the engagement of a farmer for whom she has feelings. Starring Carli Norris

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Jon Kay and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Tinker Bell and the Legend of the NeverBeast (2014)
programme.desc: One of Tinker Bell's friends meets a large hairy monster, and discovers he is not as fearsome as he looks. However, she has a hard time convincing the other fairies to accept him, and needs the help of her friends to protect the creature. Animated fantasy, with the voices of Mae Whitman, Ginnifer Goodwin and Rosario Dawson

programme.title: The Muppets (2011)
programme.desc: Three fans of the famous puppet variety show discover a powerful businessman is going to knock down the Muppet theatre and drill for oil on the site. They track down frontman Kermit the frog and persuade him to reunite his co-stars, so they can put on one last performance to save the venue. Comedy, starring Jason Segel, Amy Adams and Chris Cooper, with songs by Bret McKenzie (Flight of the Conchords)

programme.title: Snow Animals
programme.desc: Liz Bonnin introduces a cast of charismatic animals to reveal the remarkable strategies they use to survive, and even thrive, in the winter. Preparations start in autumn for Siberian chipmunks, which collect food in their cheek pouches to stockpile in their burrows, while caribou make an epic journey to new feeding areas away from the worst weather. With the arrival of winter, an American bobcat has to find its prey beneath deep snow, an arctic fox relies on its wits to find a meal, and a sea otter keeps its cub warm by blowing air into its coat

programme.title: Oz the Great and Powerful (2013)
programme.desc: An amoral circus illusionist is transported to a magical land, where he is mistaken for a powerful wizard prophesied to fall from the sky and save the realm from evil. His attempt to take advantage of the situation leads him into a dangerous quest involving three powerful witches. Fantasy adventure prequel to The Wizard of Oz, starring James Franco, Mila Kunis, Rachel Weisz and Michelle Williams

programme.title: Worzel Gummidge
programme.desc: City children Susan and John are spending their summer at Scatterbrook Farm, where they are amazed to discover the scarecrow of Ten Acre Field can come to life. But it seems this extraordinary new friendship has thrown the rhythm of the natural world out of kilter and now they must work to put things right. Mackenzie Crook writes, directs and stars in the first of two new dramas from 2019 based on the books by Barbara Euphan Todd - last adapted 40 years ago in the popular ITV series with Jon Pertwee. Co-starring Steve Pemberton, Rosie Cavaliero and Vicki Pepperdine

programme.title: Wallace & Gromit: A Grand Day Out
programme.desc: Nick Park's Bafta-winning film which introduced the animated inventor and his faithful canine sidekick to the world. Wallace is distraught when his cheese supplies run out, so he decides to take a trip to the moon. All he needs to do is build himself a rocket. With the voice of Peter Sallis

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman host a children's TV-themed edition of the unorthodox quiz show, in which contestants must come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. Playing this time are Paul Chuckle, Dani Harmer, Gwyneth Powell, George Christopher, Hacker, Dodge, Andy Day and Cerrie Burnell

programme.title: Marcus Rashford: Feeding Britain's Children
programme.desc: A look at the Manchester United and England star's campaign calling for children from poor families in England to get free meals during the school holidays. The 23 year-old striker speaks openly about his personal connection with hunger, his experiences of going without food as a child and the sacrifices his family had to make. The film also hears from those closest to him, including his mum Mel, his brothers Dwaine and Dane and his friends, while Marcus meets some of the families directly affected by the issue

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ian is living in fear - and it seems he is right to be worried when a loud bang goes off in the Vic, leaving him cowering under a table. Tina is terrified when the police take her down to the station for questioning, Karen is distraught when Gray says he wants the kids for Christmas and Kathy is determined to have all her family together

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: A funeral for a pet bird inspires Geraldine to transform the church into an ark and hold a special service for the animals of the village - but David is far from impressed by her stunt. Comedy, starring Dawn French and Gary Waldhorn

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: Geraldine anounces the winner of the Dibley mask competition, Hugo sets a new record as he delivers a sermon and the vicar reflects on a challenging year as Christmas draws near. The last of three mini-episodes of Richard Curtis's comedy, starring Dawn French and James Fleet

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef Christmas Cook-Off
programme.desc: John Torode and Gregg Wallace return for the first of two festive specials as they invite memorable celebrities from previous series back to the MasterChef kitchen. Broadcaster and journalist Janet Street-Porter, actor Christopher Biggins, radio presenter Dev Griffin and TV personality Vicky Pattison compete against each other to see who can take home the title of Christmas champion

programme.title: Mission: Impossible (1996)
programme.desc: Secret agents suspect there is a traitor in their organisation when their latest assignment ends in disaster. The sole survivor is accused of being the mole, forcing him to go on the run to clear his name. However, in the process, he discovers a former Russian spy is selling information about the world's leading undercover operatives. Action thriller based on the 1960s TV series, starring Tom Cruise, Jon Voight and Ving Rhames

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: An extended compilation of highlights from the satirical quiz, looking back at the big stories of the year with team captains Paul Merton and Ian Hislop and a variety of guest hosts and panellists

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host chats to George Clooney about his new sci-fi movie The Midnight Sky. Michael Sheen and David Tennant discuss playing bickering locked-down actors in the second series of Staged, while Oscar-winner Viola Davis talks about American drama Ma Rainey's Black Bottom. Vanessa Kirby reflects on starring in the searing domestic drama Pieces of a Woman and Michael Ball and Alfie Boe take to the stage to perform Have Yourself a Merry Little Christmas

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Jon Kay and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: CBeebies Presents Christmas in Storyland
programme.desc: A special festive show featuring the stars of CBeebies, in which a delivery elf must take a stocking full of presents to the empress of Storyland, encountering magical creatures along the way - including a yodeling yeti. Starring , Andy Day, Justin Fletcher and Gemma Hunt

programme.title: Muppets Most Wanted (2014)
programme.desc: The Muppets prepare to head off on a world tour, unaware that Kermit has been replaced by an evil genius who is his exact double. As the dastardly interloper uses the gang's performances as cover for an international crime spree, the real Kermit is left languishing in a Russian prison. Comedy, starring Ricky Gervais, Tina Fey and Ty Burrell

programme.title: Kung Fu Panda: Secrets of the Masters
programme.desc: Spin-off animation. Po and the Furious Five uncover the legend of three of kung fu's greatest heroes: Master Thundering Rhino, Master Storming Ox and Master Croc. Starring Jack Black, Angelina Jolie, Dustin Hoffman and Seth Rogen

programme.title: Spy in the Snow
programme.desc: Undercover cameras reveal how living in the snow tests animals to their limit. In New Zealand, cameras follow a group of kea parrots, revealing how friendship is at the heart of the lives of these sociable and highly intelligent birds. In the North Pole, cameras get up close to play-fighting polar bears, while on the other side of the planet at the South Pole, footage reveals the parental devotion that emperor penguins have as they try to secure the survival of their offspring and face some of the harshest conditions on Earth. In Tasmania, the spy wallaby finds love - discovering along the way an interesting courtship technique among wombats

programme.title: Clash of the Titans (1981)
programme.desc: A hero of ancient Greece vows to save a princess from being sacrificed to a ferocious sea monster. However, to defeat the creature he must first embark on a journey into the underworld and seize the head of the monstrous Medusa, whose gaze turns men to stone. Mythical adventure, starring Harry Hamlin, Laurence Olivier, Maggie Smith and Judi Bowker, with special effects by Ray Harryhausen

programme.title: Worzel Gummidge
programme.desc: Second of two family dramas from 2019 about a walking, talking scarecrow. The Green Man, who creates scarecrows and is the keeper of scarecrow lore, arrives at the farm - and is not at all happy that Worzel is consorting with humans. Meanwhile, Susan and George go to visit Lady Bloomsbury Barton, who is holding a fete with a scarecrow competition that Worzel is determined to win. What will his most competitive rival, Soggy Bogart, make of it all? Mackenzie Crook and Michael Palin star

programme.title: Wallace & Gromit: The Wrong Trousers
programme.desc: Nick Park's Oscar-winning animation featuring the bumbling inventor and his faithful dog, whose lives are turned upside down after foolishly offering board and lodgings to villainous penguin Feathers McGraw. With the voice of Peter Sallis

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a special family edition of the general knowledge quiz, with contestants Ade Edmondson, Beattie Edmondson, Helen Richardson-Walsh, Kate Richardson-Walsh, Dr Ranj Singh, Min Singh, Mark Steel and Elliot Steel

programme.title: The Goes Wrong Show
programme.desc: The Cornley Polytechnic Drama Society put on a Nativity play in this festive special - but with the BBC refusing to fund their disastrous productions, they are forced to seek corporate sponsorship. As usual, the show descends into chaos with an ingenious pop-up book proving a danger to cast and crew alike, the two actors playing the donkey falling out, and the baby Jesus being eaten by a sheep

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The police tell Ian that Tina has been taken in for questioning, although his relief doesn't last long when another vicious incident leaves him terrified. Max is determined to start a new life with Linda and makes her an offer. Karen feels depressed about facing her first Christmas without Chantelle and comes to a decision, while Honey discovers that the fridge is broken, meaning she will have to cancel the community event

programme.title: Spider-Man: Homecoming (2017)
programme.desc: Peter Parker balances life as an ordinary high-school student with his superhero alter-ego Spider-Man, while waiting in vain to join his mentor Iron Man on a world-saving mission. Determined to prove he is ready for bigger challenges, he investigates a spree of crimes committed with advanced weapons - leading him into a battle with a ruthless winged enemy on the hunt for priceless technology. Superhero adventure, starring Tom Holland, Michael Keaton and Robert Downey Jr

programme.title: Collateral (2004)
programme.desc: A Los Angeles taxi driver picks up a sharp-suited fare and is shocked when a dead body almost plunges through his windscreen during the first stop on the journey. The enigmatic passenger then reveals he is a hitman with four more contracts to carry out before the night is over, and forces the driver to help him. Michael Mann's thriller, starring Tom Cruise and Jamie Foxx

programme.title: Cuckoo
programme.desc: It's Christmas time for the Thompsons in Lichfield, and Lorna is full of yuletide spirit - in more ways than one. Ben and Dale both want to make Rachel's holiday special - and Ken gets stuck in the middle. Comedy, starring Greg Davies and Helen Baxendale

programme.title: Bad Education
programme.desc: Mr Fraser appoints Alfie as director for the Christmas production, and decides to cast the school bully as the lead in a combination of Robocop and the Nutcracker, called Robocracker. Meanwhile, Mitchell tries to lure Alfie's estranged mother from her home in Spain as a surprise, and Miss Gulliver attempts to persuade her class to volunteer in a soup kitchen

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Crackerjack!
programme.desc: Sam and Mark host a bumper edition of filled with festive fun. Naga Munchetty and Charlie Stayt play an extra-messy game of Splatterjack, and there is also music from Becky Hill

programme.title: Nativity! (2009)
programme.desc: A primary school teacher is given the job of directing a nativity play and is determined to outdo a rival from a nearby private school. When he dishonestly claims his ex-girlfriend is a Hollywood producer and is coming to see the show with an eye on making a film, he finds himself becoming a local celebrity. Comedy, starring Martin Freeman and Ashley Jensen

programme.title: Donkey's Caroling Christmas-tacular
programme.desc: Animated short following the efforts of lovable Shrek character Donkey, who tries to get his friends in a festive mood by singing a series of Christmas songs. Featuring the voices of Eddie Murphy, Mike Myers and Cameron Diaz

programme.title: Animal Park Christmas Special
programme.desc: Highlights of Ben, Kate and Jean's week at Longleat Safari Park during the festive period, including the surprise birth of a baby aardvark, and a Christmas tree arriving in the elephant house. Elsewhere, keepers face a tough decision as they try to forge a new relationship with cheetahs Carl and Wilma

programme.title: The NeverEnding Story (1984)
programme.desc: A lonely boy, neglected by his father and bullied at school, finds comfort in a mysterious book about a young hero's quest to save his world from destruction. However, as he reads the tale, he begins to become part of the story. Fantasy adventure based on Michael Edne's novel, starring Barret Oliver, Noah Hathaway, Gerald McRaney, Moses Gunn and Patricia Hayes

programme.title: Merry Madagascar
programme.desc: The animals deliver Santa's presents after he crash-lands on the island, but they plan to use the good deed as an excuse to get back to New York City. Animated adventure, with the voices of Ben Stiller, Chris Rock, David Schwimmer and Jada Pinkett Smith

programme.title: Finding Dory (2016)
programme.desc: Young clownfish Nemo and his dad Marlin set out on an underwater adventure to help their forgetful friend Dory find her parents, meeting the grouchy but well-meaning octopus Hank along the way. Pixar animated sequel, featuring the voices of Ellen DeGeneres, Albert Brooks and Ed O'Neill

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a star-studded festive edition of the general knowledge quiz in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. The contestants are Dianne Buswell, Max George, Sally Phillips, Milton Jones, John Barnes MBE, Dave Beasant, Jodie Kidd and Lisa Snowdon. Plus, a special performance from Paul Heaton and Jacqui Abbott

programme.title: Mary Berry Saves Christmas
programme.desc: Mary comes to the aid of three members of the public who claim to be hopeless in the kitchen. She gives them a crash course in creating a surprise festive feast for their families, with simple recipes that capture all of the essence and flavours of the season. Helping out in the kitchen will be celebrity sous chefs Jay Blades, Tom Allen and Angela Scanlon

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Experts try to restore family heirlooms and treasured possessions to their former glory in time for Christmas. Among the items is a Victorian version of a jukebox called a Polyphon, that has rusted into silence. It moves sisters Ann and Kathryn to tears when they hear it play the family's favourite carol for the first time in decades

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Christmas comes to the haunted household of Button House in this festive special, and Alison and Mike prepare to host a family Christmas for the first time. Alison finds her phantom housemates less than filled with festive cheer, and learns that Christmas is never easy when you're dead. Mike comes up with a dubious plan to get his parents into the holiday spirit, while Julian is confronted by a new arrival, who forces him to relive memories of his life and come to terms with his past misdeeds

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef Christmas Cook-Off
programme.desc: In this festive special, John Torode and Gregg Wallace invite some of the most memorable celebrities from series past - Craig Revel Horwood, Amar Latif, Crissy Rock and Spencer Matthews - back to the MasterChef kitchen. The foursome compete against each other in a series of challenges in the hopes of taking home the title of Christmas champion

programme.title: King Gary
programme.desc: In this festive special, Gray is looking forward to the Butterchurn Crescent Christmas lights display, which he hopes will brighten up the end of a difficult year - only for the event to be cancelled due to expense. Having landed a lucrative new contract at work, he takes it upon himself to save the lights - and Christmas

programme.title: Dolly Parton's Christmas Concert
programme.desc: Dolly Parton performs a Christmas concert featuring a host of her favourite festive songs

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: The Rev Geraldine Granger delivers online sermons for her parishioners during the pandemic, beginning by explaining the difference between metres and miles when it comes to social distancing. A compilation of the 2020 editions of Richard Curtis's comedy, starring Dawn French

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Dan Walker is joined by former players Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present highlights of the 15th round of fixtures and look ahead to forthcoming contests

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's The Wheel
programme.desc: The comedian hosts the game show, as contestants take on a colossal spinning wheel and answer questions in the hope of securing a cash prize

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Mountain Vets
programme.desc: Nuala spends the morning pregnancy-testing the livestock on her family farm, where her father and brother tend 200 dairy cows. Jennie attends Minnie the Jack Russell, who has delivered one of her pups, with more still to come. However, Minnie's labour has slowed dangerously and Jennie has to act fast if she is to save the lives of the rest of the litter - leading to a difficult decision for owner Jamie. Meanwhile, an emergency case leaves Darren delivering two sets of twins

programme.title: Mary Berry's Simple Comforts
programme.desc: In the final episode, Mary travels to the Highlands. Surrounded by magical scenery, snow-capped mountains and frost covered forests, Mary makes delicious food to warm her up after a day out in the cold, including a sausage and pepper hotpot, simple twice-baked souffles, and an onion and sage open puff pastry tart

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament, presented by Alicia McCarthy

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: Five pairs of competitors journey around the globe, carrying only limited cash and unable to get on a plane or use mobile phones and credit cards. In the first edition, the teams set off from Mexico City, hoping to reach the first of seven checkpoints - Copan Ruinas in Honduras. Each team adopts a different strategy, with married couple Jen and Rob quickly deciding on a route via Belize, but confusion on the Metro leads to a delay

programme.title: 12 Puppies and Us
programme.desc: Following the stories of puppies and their owners, including border collie Meg, who has her work cut out for her as she trains to be a sheepdog. Cameras also follow great dane Shelby as he joins a young family which already includes Mr Cooper, a three-year-old dachshund

programme.title: Wild Tales from the Village
programme.desc: Documentary following the wildlife near a remote French village, including red squirrels, wild boar, dormice and stone martens. As the animals struggle to survive the winter, they take full advantage of living so close to unsuspecting humans in their search for food, while trying not to be detected. Narrated by Tcheky Karyo

programme.title: Viva Las Vegas (1964)
programme.desc: A charismatic racing driver arrives in the city to prepare for its first grand prix. Unfortunately, his car has no engine, so to make the money to buy one he takes a job as a casino waiter. However, he loses focus when he falls in love, leaving a rival with the means to steal the race and his girl. Musical, with Elvis Presley, Ann-Margret, Cesare Danova and William Demarest

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross raids the archives for the most memorable buys over the past 20 years, with early appearances from David Dickinson, Tim Wonnacott, Kate Bliss and Phil Serrell

programme.title: Nature's Weirdest Events
programme.desc: Chris Packham explores some of the most bizarre and extraordinary natural events on the planet. This episode visits the town brought to a standstill by a swarm of flying insects, meets the polar bears whose bizarre friendships shocked a community and the sleepless residents kept awake by a lover's deafening serenade

programme.title: Doctor Zhivago (1965)
programme.desc: A married Russian physician falls in love with another woman, but as their passionate affair plays out against the raging backdrop of the First World War and the Bolshevik revolution, he is torn between his desires and his loyalty to his wife. David Lean's epic drama based on Boris Pasternak's novel, starring Omar Sharif, Julie Christie, Geraldine Chaplin, Rod Steiger, Alec Guinness, Ralph Richardson, Tom Courtenay and Klaus Kinski

programme.title: A Very Country Christmas
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison and Angellica Bell celebrate Christmas traditions from three distinct parts of Britain. They begin by joining Advent preparations in the Highlands, meeting a Christmas tree farmer, a candlemaker and a woman who makes a traditional Scottish delicacy

programme.title: Some Mothers Do 'Ave 'Em
programme.desc: Festive special from 1975. In the run-up to Christmas, Frank loses yet another job but hopes to make up for it by appearing on a DIY programme - only to blow more than a fuse with his efforts. Undeterred, he sets out to pass his driving test on the 10th attempt, and if speeding up an airport runway is not bad enough, things take a turn for the worse when the examiner steps into the car and discovers there is something fishy about Frank. Michael Crawford and Michele Dotrice star, with a guest appearance by David Jacobs

programme.title: Butterflies
programme.desc: Ria thinks of Christmas as a time of truth - but finds herself having to do everything she hates the most. A 1979 festive special of the vintage comedy by Carla Lane, starring Wendy Craig, Geoffrey Palmer and Nicholas Lyndhurst

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace and Cherry Healey find out how favourite festive party foods are made. Gregg visits an enormous factory in Nottingham where 200,000 canapes are produced every 24 hours. Cherry discovers fail-safe scientific methods for cooking the perfect Christmas roast, turning her oven into a turkey sauna by popping ice cubes into a baking tray. Historian Ruth Goodman looks into the tradition of kissing under the mistletoe and also investigates how the period of advent, traditionally associated with fasting, came to be connected with chocolate-packed calendars

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys invites four more contestants to brave the black chair, asking them questions on the specialist subjects of Lyndon B Johnson, A Song of Ice and Fire, seabirds, and children's TV, before they are tested on their general knowledge

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts another second-round match as the Sliders and the Puzzle Hunters compete for a place in the quarter-finals. The contestants must use patience, lateral thinking and sheer inspiration to make connections between four things that may appear at first not to be linked, with one set of clues consisting of Andromeda, Fake, Zachariah, and Vendetta

programme.title: Christmas University Challenge
programme.desc: Teams of distinguished graduates compete on behalf of their former universities in the academic quiz's festive incarnation, with the opening first-round match seeing Christ's Cambridge take on St John's Oxford. Among those facing questions from host Jeremy Paxman are painter and broadcaster Lachlan Goudie and poet Helen Mort for Christ's, while the St John's team includes BBC business editor Simon Jack and author John Lanchester

programme.title: Upstart Crow
programme.desc: David Mitchell and Gemma Whelan star in a two-hander Christmas special, in which Will and Kate find themselves spending Christmas in lockdown far from their families as the plague ravages London. Kate fills her days in isolation with an endless series of projects, Will has more pressing concerns - with James I now on the throne, he needs to come up with a masterpiece to retain favour at the royal court and avoid execution. But while he may have time on his hands to write, inspiration is in very short supply

programme.title: Inside No 9
programme.desc: Trevor and Julia always want to make Christmas really special for their family, but this year money is so tight that things are not going to be easy. As each door of the Advent calendar opens, everyone will have to do their bit to make the festive season happen. Darkly comic anthology written by and starring The League of Gentlemen and Psychoville's Steve Pemberton and Reece Shearsmith, with guest appearances by Debbie Rush, Gaby French, Bobby Schofield and Olly Hudson-Croker

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: A special edition of the topical comedy show featuring seasonal material, fond favourites and previously unseen bits, with host Dara O Briain and regular panellist Hugh Dennis

programme.title: The Revenant (2015)
programme.desc: A frontiersman leading a hunting party through the wilderness is mauled by a bear, and a travelling companion who pledged to stay with him until help comes kills his son and leaves him for dead. Against all odds, the wounded man survives his injuries and embarks on a gruelling quest for revenge that takes him through a harsh landscape. Western, starring an Oscar-winning Leonardo DiCaprio, with Tom Hardy and Domhnall Gleeson

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team explores the Stour Estuary in Essex, which has just become the first extension in 30 years to an Area of Outstanding Natural Beauty. Matt Baker and Margherita Taylor are on a quest to find little critters - from bugs and beetles to crabs and worms. Plus, Adam Henson welcomes early lambs to his farm in the Cotswolds, and Tom Heap investigates a tale of two rural high streets in lockdown

programme.title: Emin/Munch: Between the Clock and the Bed
programme.desc: This winter sees the opening of The Loneliness of the Soul, a new exhibition at London's Royal Academy, showcasing the work of Tracey Emin, alongside paintings by Edvard Munch. Munch's work has long inspired Emin, from her early video piece Homage to Edvard Munch and All My Dead Children to her monumental new nine-metre high bronze sculpture, The Mother, set to be installed outside Oslo's new Munch Museum next spring. This documentary charts the affinities between Munch's art and Emin's, exploring the ways that this godfather of expressionism resonates with one of Britain's pre-eminent artists

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The contest reaches the semi-finals, in which the remaining 10 chefs must prepare a dish based on their favourite food memory. Five of the chefs will be chosen to go through to the next round, while the remaining five must face an invention test to preserve their place in the contest. This challenge involves preparing a dish for a fine dining food delivery experience in 90 minutes

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: A Very Country Christmas
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison and Angellica Bell celebrate Christmas traditions from three distinct parts of Britain. They begin by joining Advent preparations in the Highlands, meeting a Christmas tree farmer, a candlemaker and a woman who makes a traditional Scottish delicacy

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: The teams head out of Copan Ruinas in Western Honduras to their next checkpoint - Panama City. The journey sees them travel across vast swathes of Central America, passing through El Salvador, Nicaragua, Costa Rica and finally Panama. Mother and son Jo and Sam are the first to set off, and having raced hard in the previous leg, they're keen to experience more this time round. Working at a hostel in El Salvador offers some respite from the race, and gives the pair the chance to help with a turtle conservation project

programme.title: 12 Puppies and Us
programme.desc: Following the stories of first-time puppy owners. Rescue dog Elina moves in with the Orchard family in Buckinghamshire after a 2,000-mile trip from Cyprus, where she was found abandoned on the side of the road. Meanwhile, Sage the Staffordshire bull terrier cross is a massive hit with the Fianko family

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Hugh Bonneville narrates a documentary looking at the habits of the species and the skills they have developed to survive in a range of environments around the world. The film explores how bears have overcome the challenges of life - from finding food and raising their cubs to confronting rivals and habitat loss - all thanks to brains, brawn and a remarkable ability to adapt. Part of the Natural World strand

programme.title: High Society (1956)
programme.desc: Romantic complications arise when a playboy composer sets out to win back his ex-wife. However, she is about to marry another man - and the situation becomes even more difficult when a cynical reporter enters the fray. Musical comedy based on The Philadelphia Story with a score by Cole Porter, starring Bing Crosby, Frank Sinatra and Grace Kelly, with Louis Armstrong appearing as himself

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp looks back on some of the experts' best and worst buys from the past 20 years of the series

programme.title: Nature's Weirdest Events
programme.desc: Chris Packham explores some of the most bizarre and extraordinary natural events on the planet. This episode visits the town brought to a standstill by a swarm of flying insects, meets the polar bears whose bizarre friendships shocked a community and the sleepless residents kept awake by a lover's deafening serenade

programme.title: Rick Stein's Spain
programme.desc: Having spent a series travelling across Spain and sampling classic dishes from the country's rural regions, Rick Stein draws culinary inspiration from that trip to create a series of dinner-table feasts that would befit any Spanish yuletide celebration. The chef prepares clams with serrano ham and oloroso sherry, lamb-stuffed aubergines with Moorish spices, chicken with saffron and pine nuts, and orange caramel creams

programme.title: The Searchers (1956)
programme.desc: An embittered American Civil War veteran sets out to find his niece, who has been abducted by a renegade Comanche tribe. His search takes him many years, and during the long quest his travelling companion starts to wonder if he intends to rescue her or kill her. John Ford's Western, starring John Wayne, Natalie Wood, Jeffrey Hunter and Vera Miles

programme.title: A Very Country Christmas
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison and Angellica Bell visit a town in Yorkshire that inspired Charles Dickens when he was writing A Christmas Carol. They also reveal the story behind why many of us eat turkeys at Christmas, and come across a rather unusual Nativity scene

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth decides to build a dream kitchen in time for Christmas - but a series of unforeseen setbacks, culminating in Richard contracting an embarrassing fungal infection, reduces her plans to a farce. Meanwhile, Rose and Daisy make an alarming discovery in their father's room. Festive special from 1994, starring Patricia Routledge and Clive Swift

programme.title: The Good Life
programme.desc: Margo returns her entire Christmas order because the tree is too short - and she and Jerry are left with no means to celebrate after the department store fails to redeliver in time. Tom and Barbara come to the rescue, inviting them round for a Christmas of homemade paper hats and improvised party games. Seasonal special from 1977, starring Richard Briers, Penelope Keith, Paul Eddington and Felicity Kendal

programme.title: All About The Good Life
programme.desc: In !975 a new sitcom introduced viewers to the self-sufficient world of Tom and Barbara Good, who had a smallholding in their suburban back garden, much to the horror of the upwardly mobile Leadbetters next door. This documentary reveals all about the show created by Bob Larbey and John Esmonde, from the perils of filming knee-deep in mud to the ultimate accolade of performing live in front of the Queen at Television Centre. Contributors include stars Richard Briers and Penelope Keith, as well as Monty Don, Brian Sewell and John O'Farrell

programme.title: Christmas University Challenge
programme.desc: Jeremy Paxman asks the questions as teams of famous alumni from Manchester and Queen's Belfast compete for a place in the semi-finals. The Manchester team consists of actor Adrian Edmondson and war surgeon David Nott, while Queen's is represented by Kate Devlin, writer of a book on sex and robots, and poet Miriam Gamble

programme.title: Nigella's Cook, Eat, Repeat
programme.desc: Nigella Lawson prepares a festive feast with a Nordic theme for a group of friends. After a wintery walk through the woods in which she divulges her plans, Nigella gets to work on pickled red cabbage and cucumber salad, no knead black bread, smoked salmon, and Norwegian Pork ribs. A glass of pomegranate fizz accompanies the meal which is followed by the traditional Dutch New Year's Eve treat of doughnuts lightly dusted in icing sugar

programme.title: Being Bridget Jones
programme.desc: Documentary celebrating 25 years of Helen Fielding's hapless creation. The programme examines how society's attitudes to women have shifted in 25 years, along with the ways in which female writers have told their own stories since Bridget made her debut. Featuring interviews with Helen Fielding, and the friends she based the original characters on, as well as tributes from celebrity fans

programme.title: I'll Get This
programme.desc: Five celebrities go out for Christmas dinner together and between courses play a series of games, with the eventual loser having to pay for the meal. Taking part in this festive special are Strictly Come Dancing judge Shirley Ballas, comedian and TV presenter Joel Dommett, Love Island star Maura Higgins, MasterChef presenter Gregg Wallace and actor and funnyman Les Dennis

programme.title: Industry
programme.desc: Yasmin organises a party to allow the grads to blow off some steam, as the decision over who will remain with the company looms, but begins to suspect she will not be kept on after Wyndham requests a private conversation. Harper struggles to adapt to Daria's new way of doing things, and Gus starts looking for other opportunities

programme.title: Industry
programme.desc: The grads pull out all the stops to become permanent employees. Harper gets a lifeline from an unexpected source, and Yasmin weighs up the pros and cons of her conversation with Wyndham. Robert's fate looks dubious with Clement gone and Daria out to get him, and Sara offers Gus some hope for the future

programme.title: Radio 2 in Concert
programme.desc: The singer takes to the stage at the BBC Radio Theatre in London, performing festive songs from his new album The Christmas Present, including a cover version of Slade's Merry Xmas Everybody and current single Time for Change. Robbie also treats the audience to some of his biggest hits, including Feel, She's the One and Angels

programme.title: The Misadventures of Romesh Ranganathan
programme.desc: The comedian visits the Hebrides to find out if you can have a proper adventure so close to home, spending a week exploring the Islands of Skye, Harris and Lewis. Guiding Romesh through the journey is Donald MacSween, a local crofter from the far North of Lewis - a place that lies as far north as Greenland

programme.title: New Elizabethans with Andrew Marr
programme.desc: The second documentary looks at how Britain coped with the loss of empire, the dominance of America and the rise of a consolidated European superstate in the decades after the Second World War. Andrew Marr profiles some of the key figures who sought to come to terms with Britain's decline, including Earl Mountbatten of Burma, cookery writer Elizabeth David and singer and activist Bob Geldof, as well as less familiar figures like Colonel Colin 'Mad Mitch' Mitchell, and pioneering peace campaigner Helen John

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: With just eight contestants remaining in the competition, the first four chefs come together and face-off over two challenges in the hope of making it to the next round. First, the chefs are given a world class demonstration from the celebrated Michelin-starred chef Aktar Islam, before presenting a dish of their own creating for Aktar and the three judges. In the second challenge, the chefs have 90 minutes to prepare two courses of their own design, hoping to secure their place in Finals Week

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: A Very Country Christmas
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison and Angellica Bell visit a town in Yorkshire that inspired Charles Dickens when he was writing A Christmas Carol. They also reveal the story behind why many of us eat turkeys at Christmas, and come across a rather unusual Nativity scene

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: The teams set off from Panama City across the Caribbean Sea, but they face an unplanned obstacle when a state of emergency is declared in Quito, Ecuador - the destination of their next checkpoint. The teams are forced to change route in mid-race and head into the remote Colombian desert. Rob and Jen make use of night buses to get ahead, Emon and Jamiul raise some money by teaching scuba diving, and Sam struggles with the pressure and needs a break

programme.title: 12 Puppies and Us
programme.desc: Lyra the Pomsky moves in with the busy Cuthbertson family in Gateshead, but after the dog chews toddler Ellie's toys, her parent call in Louise to help stop the destruction and restore harmony to the house. In Cardiff, Ailsa has to decide whether she is confident enough to let Chinese crested powderpuff Fabio run free across the beach

programme.title: Grizzly Bear Cubs and Me
programme.desc: Part one of two. Gordon Buchanan joins the Pazhetnovs, a pioneering family of biologists in Russia as they attempt to rehab grizzly bear cub orphans. There are more than 100,000 grizzlies in Russia and as humans encroach further into this vast wilderness, conflict is increasing. The Pazhetnovs have been rescuing orphaned cubs for nearly three decades and have successfully returned more than 200 of them back to the wild. Gordon arrives in February when six cubs have just been rescued. They are just five weeks old and each weigh less than a bag of sugar

programme.title: Lifeline
programme.desc: Kristin Scott Thomas makes an appeal on behalf of Winston's Wish, a charity providing support for bereaved children and their families

programme.title: Jane Eyre (2011)
programme.desc: A girl raised in misery by her cruel aunt grows up to become a governess, and accepts work at the estate of a mysterious landowner. Despite being disturbed by strange goings-on in the house, she falls in love with her brooding employer, only to discover he is concealing a terrible secret. Period drama based on Charlotte Bronte's novel, starring Mia Wasikowska, Michael Fassbender, Jamie Bell and Judi Dench

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: To celebrate Bargain Hunt's 20th birthday, Christina Trevanion tours the UK looking back at what our teams have bought over the last two decades

programme.title: Nature's Weirdest Events
programme.desc: Chris Packham investigates unusual natural events, with the help of eyewitness accounts, scientists and footage of the occurrences

programme.title: Nigella's Cook, Eat, Repeat
programme.desc: Nigella Lawson prepares a festive feast with a Nordic theme for a group of friends. After a wintery walk through the woods in which she divulges her plans, Nigella gets to work on pickled red cabbage and cucumber salad, no knead black bread, smoked salmon, and Norwegian Pork ribs. A glass of pomegranate fizz accompanies the meal which is followed by the traditional Dutch New Year's Eve treat of doughnuts lightly dusted in icing sugar

programme.title: Casablanca (1942)
programme.desc: The American expat owner of a Moroccan nightclub is reunited with his old flame during the Second World War. Now married to a Czech freedom fighter, she needs his help to get them safely out of the country before they are captured by the Nazis - but the former lovers also have to wrestle the temptation to rekindle their simmering romance. Drama, with Humphrey Bogart, Ingrid Bergman, Claude Rains and Paul Henreid

programme.title: A Very Country Christmas
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison and Angellica Bell celebrate Cornish Christmas traditions, visiting the cathedral where Nine Lessons and Carols first began - with the intention of getting people out of pubs and into church

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: Young Mr Grace celebrates his birthday, so the staff decide to organise a cabaret in his honour. Another chance to see the 1978 Christmas special of the comedy, starring John Inman and Mollie Sugden

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: The platoon challenges a rival regiment to a gruelling duel, involving map-reading, initiative tests, river-crossing and field-fighting, to prove they are fitter and better trained than any other troop. Christmas special of the comedy with the bumbling Home Guard of Walmington-on-Sea from 1971, starring Arthur Lowe, John Le Mesurier and Ian Lavender

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Bob Mortimer returns to his childhood roots in Middlesbrough to fish the River Esk and Tees with his friend and angling mentor Paul Whitehouse. As well as trying to catch the glorious grayling, Bob is also going to try to ignite the joy of Christmas in Paul, who thinks the magic of the season has been long lost to commercialism

programme.title: The Magical World of Julia Donaldson
programme.desc: Celebration of the life and work of the bestselling children's author, with animated sequences by long-term illustrator Axel Scheffler bringing her story to life. The film reveals the surprising stories behind iconic characters like the Gruffalo, Zog and Stick Man, and explores how her simple but poignant handling of themes of love, loss, fear and tyranny appeal to children and adults alike

programme.title: Christmas University Challenge
programme.desc: Jeremy Paxman asks the questions as teams of famous alumni from Courtauld Institute of Art and Goldsmiths, University of London, compete for a place in the semi-finals. The Courtauld team, which feature artist Jeremy Deller and writer and poet Lavinia Greenlaw, go up against the team from Goldsmiths, which features Hairy Biker Dave Myers and author Helen Cross

programme.title: Motherland
programme.desc: Christmas special of the sitcom in which Amanda organises her annual festive soiree, complete with a strict code of conduct on when and where to drink mulled wine and an evil Santa. Meg is the only other mother to earn an invite, but Julia, Kevin and Liz all tag along anyway. For Julia, the party is a welcome distraction from hosting her in-laws, while Liz is trying to ignore Christmas altogether and Kevin is single-handedly stuffing, wrapping, roasting, parboiling, laying up, washing up and decorating everything he can lay his hands on

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig hosts a festive edition of the panel game, joined by Chris McCausland, Justin Moorhouse, Holly Walsh and regular Alan Davies. The panel is posed questions on topics like how to get a reindeer into a submarine, why the children of Dijon rebelled against Santa Claus and what the immaculate conception really means. Along the way, the show sets two Christmas-themed world records

programme.title: Phantom Thread (2017)
programme.desc: Celebrated British dressmaker Reynolds Woodcock falls in love with Alma, a shy but strong-willed waitress who refuses to conform to his meticulous way of living. Paul Thomas Anderson's Oscar-winning drama, starring Daniel Day-Lewis in his final role before retirement, alongside Vicky Krieps and Lesley Manville

programme.title: The Noughties
programme.desc: Angela Scanlon is joined by two guests to take a look at the year's highlights

programme.title: Saving Britain's Pubs with Tom Kerridge
programme.desc: Coronavirus is spreading and the British public are told to stay away from pubs. Tom and the landlords he has been supporting face a moral dilemma. Do they keep trying to trade or do they close their doors to fight the virus? However, on March 20 the decision is taken out of their hands when the Prime Minister tells all pubs to close until further notice. Tom started this journey to save four pubs from going under, now every pub in Britain, his own included, find themselves in a fight for survival

programme.title: Waterhole: Africa's Animal Oasis
programme.desc: During the hottest time of year, the waterhole becomes busier in the cool of night. Using remote cameras, the team uncovers the new dynamics of the period following sunset. Hyena move into the area, but they are so elusive that it is hard to know how big the clan is. Their arrival has a dramatic effect on other predators in the area, and no lions or leopards are spotted while they are around. Ella Al-Shamahi discovers how the increased footfall has changed the shape of the waterhole and how, as temperatures rise, the waterhole residents' behaviour is also changing

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The second group of four face two tough tests in a bid to make it through to finals week. First they receive a masterclass with Michelin-starred chef Andrew Wong, who demonstrates two dishes - dim sum and crispy chilli beef. They must then produce two dishes, emulating Andrew's techniques, with the judges' favourite receiving a pass through to next week. the remaining three must serve up a two-course signature menu, after which Monica Galetti, Gregg Wallace and Marcus Wareing will decide who else deserves a place in the final

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The Two Ronnies Sketchbook
programme.desc: Ronnie Corbett and Ronnie Barker reminisce from behind the famous desk about their all-time favourite sketches, including Home Rule for Ruislip, Crossed Lines and the Phantom Raspberry Blower. Lisa Stansfield provides an interlude to the comic mayhem with a performance of I've Got the World on a String

programme.title: The Joy of Painting: Winter Specials
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross paints a scene featuring a cabin and shed nestled among snowdrifts

programme.title: Charles Dickens and the Invention of Christmas
programme.desc: Griff Rhys Jones reveals how Charles Dickens virtually invented the idea of a traditional family Christmas through one of his books, A Christmas Carol. When it was published in 1843, the story of Ebenezer Scrooge captured the imagination of Victorian Britain, and boosted the new craze for the festive season at a time when the idea of Santa Claus, mistletoe and crackers were relatively new

programme.title: Oliver Twist
programme.desc: Part two of two. Sikes escapes back to London with a badly wounded Oliver, while Mr Bumble declares his love for Mrs Corney - and captivated by the promise of social advancement, she agrees to marry him. Later, Nancy stumbles on a secret meeting, during which she overhears Monks telling Fagin he wants Oliver dead in two days

programme.title: The 12 Drinks of Christmas
programme.desc: Comedy actor and Pointless host Alexander Armstrong and his journalist brother-in-law Giles Coren get together to select 12 different drinks for the festive period. Their choices range from the best Christmas Eve cocktail to the wine to serve with lunch, as well as brandy, punch and the ultimate eggnog

programme.title: How Quizzing Got Cool: TV's Brains of Britain
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell, Judith Keppel, Chris Tarrant, Mark Labbett and Nicholas Parsons help Ben Miller trace the origins of the pro-quizzers; quiz show veterans who have emerged from the crowd of quiz aficionados in recent years to achieve fame and fortune for their general knowledge prowess

programme.title: Mark Kermode's Secrets of Cinema
programme.desc: Mark Kermode celebrates one of the most perennial of all movie genres - the Christmas film. He unwraps a glittering selection of festive cinematic treats, from Hollywood blockbusters to family favourites, revealing the techniques and storytelling secrets that make them so successful. Touching on styles ranging from fairy-tale fantasy to high-octane action, family drama to horror, Mark considers how the best of these films are able to capture something magical - the Christmas spirit. But how do they do it?

programme.title: The Joy of Painting: Winter Specials
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross paints a scene featuring a cabin and shed nestled among snowdrifts

programme.title: Oliver Twist
programme.desc: Part two of two. Sikes escapes back to London with a badly wounded Oliver, while Mr Bumble declares his love for Mrs Corney - and captivated by the promise of social advancement, she agrees to marry him. Later, Nancy stumbles on a secret meeting, during which she overhears Monks telling Fagin he wants Oliver dead in two days

programme.title: James May: The Reassembler
programme.desc: In a festive special, James puts together a Hornby Flying Scotsman, his favourite childhood Christmas present, which he was given in 1972 and sent him on to a path of mechanical intrigue and reassembly. Laid out in all its 138 tiny component parts, James lovingly reassembles the train as well as his memories of Christmases and toys past

programme.title: The Joy of Painting: Winter Specials
programme.desc: Bob Ross demonstrates why winter scenes do not have to be cold, depicting the sun's warmth to turn the chill of winter into a golden day

programme.title: Yes Minister
programme.desc: The seasonal festivities at the Department of Administrative Affairs are overshadowed when the prime minister announces his intention to retire, causing a mad scramble for his job - and Hacker must find out if he has what it takes to climb to the top of the greasy pole. Festive special of the comedy from 1984, starring Paul Eddington and Nigel Hawthorne

programme.title: Mother Love
programme.desc: Andrew Davies' Bafta-winning 1989 adaptation of Domini Taylor's novel, starring Diana Rigg. Wedding bells sound for Kit and Angela - but Kit's mother's hatred for his father, her estranged husband, threatens their future happiness. With James Wilby and David McCallum

programme.title: Face to Face
programme.desc: Jeremy Isaacs presents an interview with the actress from 1997, which reflects on her rise to fame in the 1960s and award-winning theatrical career, including her acclaimed leading roles in Medea, Mother Courage and Who's Afraid of Virginia Woolf?

programme.title: Lucy Worsley's Christmas Carol Odyssey
programme.desc: Lucy explores the tradition of festive hymns, revealing that there is much more to the best-loved carols than meets the eye, and how they reveal the history of Christmas itself. In the ancient past, pagan fertility ritual the wassail gave rise to door-to-door singing, and was also an integral part of an older midwinter festival adopted by Christianity when it came to Britain, and was rebranded as Christmas

programme.title: The Last Igloo
programme.desc: Documentary following a day in the life of an Inuit hunter in Greenland, who builds one of the traditional ice dwellings while fishing through the sea ice. While igloos are no longer used as permanent homes, the traditional skills behind their construction are still used in building temporary shelters in the wilderness. The film captures this ingenious craft, which may be lost to the world as the ice sheet disappears

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1984
programme.desc: The Christmas Day edition, featuring hits including Two Tribes by Frankie Goes to Hollywood, What Is Love? by Howard Jones, The Reflex by Duran Duran, I Won't Let The Sun Go Down On Me by Nik Kershaw, The War Song by Culture Club, Doctor Doctor by Thompson Twins, I Should Have Known Better by Jim Diamond, Wake Me Up Before You Go-Go by Wham!, Love Of The Common People by Paul Young, Careless Whisper by George Michael and Do They Know It's Christmas? by Band Aid

programme.title: The Joy of Painting: Winter Specials
programme.desc: Bob Ross demonstrates why winter scenes do not have to be cold, depicting the sun's warmth to turn the chill of winter into a golden day

programme.title: The Two Ronnies Sketchbook
programme.desc: More of the renowned duo's favourite madcap moments, including the Squash sketch, Identical Dialogue Party, Real Water, Towels/Tiles and Rhyming Vicar, plus Ronnie Corbett's monologue Green Books and another helping of the Phantom Raspberry Blower. Renee Olstead is the musical guest, performing Midnight at the Oasis

programme.title: The Joy of Painting: Winter Specials
programme.desc: Bob Ross demonstrates how to paint snow-covered trees and shrubs dressed in finest lace among the soft glows of winter

programme.title: Sacred Music
programme.desc: Simon Russell Beale travels through Italy, Britain, Germany and Austria to explore how the music of Christmas has evolved in response to changing ideas about the Nativity. His journey spans two millennia, from a fragment of papyrus preserving the earliest known piece of Christian music through to the stories behind Hark! The Herald Angels Sing, Silent Night and In the Bleak Midwinter, as well as the work of one of today's most popular Christmas composers, John Rutter. Music is performed by Harry Christophers and his choir The Sixteen

programme.title: Mother Love
programme.desc: Andrew Davies' Bafta-winning 1989 adaptation of Domini Taylor's novel, starring Diana Rigg. Furious that Alex has moved on after the death of his wife, Helena must deal with the next betrayal. With James Wilby and David McCallum

programme.title: Mark Lawson Talks To
programme.desc: The arts critic interviews the actress, who originally found fame as Emma Peel in TV series The Avengers, before starring in films including On Her Majesty's Secret Service and The Hospital, as well as developing a reputation as one of Britain's leading stage performers. She discusses her childhood in India, her mixed feelings about being regarded as a sex symbol, and the skills that have enabled her to continue working into her seventies

programme.title: Carole King and Friends at Christmas
programme.desc: The singer-songwriter performs festive favourites at a concert that also features Sheffield crooner Richard Hawley, retro vocal trio the Puppini Sisters, Brighton group the Mummers, and New York jazz vocalist Gregory Porter. Among the songs on the bill are Have Yourself a Merry Little Christmas, Silent Night, Let It Snow, White Christmas and The Christmas Song

programme.title: Country Christmas
programme.desc: Festive performances by the biggest names in country music, including Dan + Shay, Darius Rucker and Lindsey Stirling, Florida Georgia Line, Gabby Barrett, Kelsea Ballerini, Lady A, Little Big Town, Tim McGraw and host Thomas Rhett

programme.title: The Joy of Painting: Winter Specials
programme.desc: Bob Ross demonstrates how to paint snow-covered trees and shrubs dressed in finest lace among the soft glows of winter

programme.title: James May: The Reassembler
programme.desc: In a festive special, James puts together a Hornby Flying Scotsman, his favourite childhood Christmas present, which he was given in 1972 and sent him on to a path of mechanical intrigue and reassembly. Laid out in all its 138 tiny component parts, James lovingly reassembles the train as well as his memories of Christmases and toys past

His journey spans two millennia, from a fragment of papyrus preserving the earliest known piece of Christian music through to the stories behind Hark! The Herald Angels Sing, Silent Night and In the Bleak Midwinter, as well as the work of one of today's most popular Christmas composers, John Rutter. Music is performed by Harry Christophers and his choir The Sixteen

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: DW gets jealous when Grandma Thora becomes the most popular babysitter in town

programme.title: Shaun the Sheep
programme.desc: The Farmer goes on holiday, confident he has left the farm in safe hands. However, the animals quickly learn that his replacement is only interested in looking after himself

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Revival of the classic cartoon about the adventures of a crimebusting rodent and his sidekick Penfold. With the voices of Alexander Armstrong and Kevin Eldon

programme.title: Hetty Feather
