programme.desc: In Hampshire, Drew Pritchard and Paul Cowland uncover a rare 1960s MGC, but clash over how to restyle it. In Dorset, they fall for an '80s BMW 635 Coupe left in a barn for 15 years

programme.title: American Chopper
programme.desc: A build begins for a mining company and a bike is unveiled for a renewable energy business. Meanwhile, Paul Jr and Paul Sr hold a private meeting

programme.title: Aircrash Confidential
programme.desc: Landing without power, running out of fuel or birds flying into the engine are all a test of pilot skill and nerve. This instalment looks at how pilots cope with emergency landings

programme.title: Mighty Ships
programme.desc: The operation behind the Africa Mercy, the world's largest NGO hospital ship that travels to the world's poorest regions to deliver care to thousands of people

programme.title: How It's Made
programme.desc: The manufacture of objects such as rubber gloves and aircraft cabinets, and how processes have developed over the years

programme.title: How It's Made
programme.desc: The processes involved in the creation of powder horns, hand-crafted moulds, pierogies and inner tubes

programme.title: Chef vs Corner Shop
programme.desc: Chizzy Akudolu hosts the contest in Somerset, as local boy and chef Dean Edwards summons all his experience, uses all his skill and finds a bit of good luck to try and defeat the line-up of celebrity chefs and the local challenger who await. Dishes include mizo-glazed aubergine with parsnip puree, chard shallots and puffed rice, chicken and artichoke ravioli and deep-fried artichoke and a caramelised pineapple and ginger cake with salted caramel sauce and smoked whipped cream

programme.title: Junior Bake Off
programme.desc: Harry Hill hosts as young cooks take part in a culinary competition to determine which of them is Britain's best junior baker. Judges Prue Leith and Liam Charles oversee proceedings in the opening heat as the first 10 of the 20 contenders enter the tent to tackle two challenges. Baking knowledge and jam-making skills are put to the test in a tricky first Technical in which the bakers are asked to follow Prue's surprise recipe for a tea party classic. This is followed by impressive Showstopper cakes themed around the bakers' favourite school subjects

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Edinburgh couple David and Venus share a passion for Portugal, so would like a holiday home in the Algarve. Jasmine Harman lines up five properties for their £95,000 budget

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Summer Sun
programme.desc: Jasmine Harman helps potential buyers Ali Harris and Steve Ashby to explore the beautiful region of Abruzzo in southern Italy, where the dream of retiring to run a B&B

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In the first of five programmes from Edinburgh, delicatessen owner and whisky connoisseur Tracy Dobbie uses alcohol to break the ice. Hoping to claim the £1,000 prize, she begins with a specially made cocktail and serves a main course containing a bottle of wine, but two of the guests are experts when it comes to drink

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The second meal in Edinburgh sees the four contestants head to wine critic Paul Dwyer's house, with rifts already starting to appear. Not everyone is delighted with the vegetarian host's meat-free Tuscan menu, and his hopes of impressing them with lectures about grapevines are soon dashed

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Texas-born Tanya Williams tries to impress her guests in Edinburgh with six courses of cuisine from the southern states of America. However, it becomes apparent she may have bitten off more than she can chew and the atmosphere around the table turns decidedly cold

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Catering manager James Gammie puts his bartending experience to good use by serving a different cocktail with each course during his dinner party in Edinburgh. Although he did not see eye to eye with fellow diner Tracy Dobbie earlier in the competition, it seems his whisky concoction may finally win her round

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: For the final meal in Edinburgh, Jennifer Crosbie hopes to impress her four guests and claim the £1,000 cash prize by improving on two dishes served up earlier in the contest. Paul Dwyer accepts her risotto might be better than his, but James Gammie is adamant his monkfish was tastier. However, the quality of food is not the host's only concern

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart cheats during an exam and finds himself hailed as a child genius, but soon discovers he is not really cut out for the academic life - especially as it means being sent away to a special school

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer reluctantly pawns the TV to pay for family counselling sessions - but Dr Marvin Monroe's shocking treatment yields unexpected results

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads for West Babylon, New York, to help the inexperienced owners of restaurant Classic American deal with their escalating debts. The chef struggles to cope with disorganised staff and an unappealing menu, but quickly realises the real problem lies in the kitchen, where a lack of leadership and communication is causing serious trouble

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef travels to Philadelphia to help failing Mexican restaurant Zocalo. Owners Greg and Mary Russell met and fell in love while they were working there, and the place held such sentimental importance to them they pooled their money and bought it. Now Gordon tries to turn things around before the floundering business ruins their marriage and tears their family apart

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien are in Surrey, where they visit a collector of Kawasaki motorbikes

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Kamlesh and Sukhwinder have been married for 42 years, and now their four children have flown the nest, wish to buy a winter home in southern Alicante, with its breathtaking views of the Mediterranean Sea and lively atmosphere. Jean Johansson shows them five properties for their £95,000 budget, but whether any will take their fancy remains to be seen

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: Architect George Clarke delves into the world of small builds, meeting people taking tiny, unpromising spaces and creating places to live, work and play

programme.title: Forrest Gump (1994)
programme.desc: A slow-witted man has a series of bizarre adventures, including becoming an American football star, Vietnam veteran, table tennis champion and millionaire businessman - but the love of his life from childhood continues to elude him. Robert Zemeckis's Oscar-winning comedy drama, starring Tom Hanks, Sally Field, Robin Wright and Gary Sinise

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts a festive edition of the words and numbers game, with Countdown regulars Susie Dent and Rachel Riley. Jon Richardson and Sarah Millican face off against Sean Lock and David Mitchell, while David O'Doherty joins Susie in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts a festive edition of the combination of the comedy panel show and the long-running words and numbers quiz, with Sean Lock and Bob Mortimer taking on Jon Richardson and Lucy Beaumont. Regular Countdown mathematician Rachel Riley and lexicographer Susie Dent are on hand to adjudicate with guests Adam Buxton and Joe Wilkinson

programme.title: World Chase Tag - USA
programme.desc: Highlights from the final in Atlanta, Georgia, as the teams compete for the title in a knockout version of the playground game

programme.title: Inside KFC at Christmas
programme.desc: Behind-the-scenes at the fast food chain in the run-up to Christmas, as a brand new festive burger is launched in a bid to keep regular customers coming back during lockdown. The programme follows the daily struggles of the franchise owners keeping their businesses going against all odds, and the staff pulling out all the stops to end the year as a success

programme.title: Devon and Cornwall
programme.desc: Documentary celebrating the festive season in Devon and Cornwall with people whose livelihood depends on this busy time of year. The first stop is Cornwall's Lizard peninsula, to meet the Richards family, who have been farming this region for more than 500 years. Cameras also follow Devon artist Luna North, whose love of the region inspires her to paint her own Christmas card

programme.title: Chef vs Corner Shop
programme.desc: Chizzy Akudolu presents the culinary challenge from Somerset, where Dean Edwards goes head-to-head with a line-up of celebrity chefs and a local challenger

programme.title: Junior Bake Off
programme.desc: Harry Hill hosts Biscuit Day in the baking challenge, and the contestants must prepare one of judge Liam Charles' favourite snacks and work on their piping skills

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: The return of the programme in which a property expert offers advice to British householders aiming to buy a home abroad. Jasmine Harman is on Spain's Costa Blanca to help Wendy and Lee find a traditional countryside property. The couple, their two children, their pet Doberman and their West African parrot are planning a permanent move to Spain and would like a four-bedroom detached house with plenty of outside space, all for their budget of £150,000. Can Jasmine find their perfect place?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Summer Sun
programme.desc: Jasmine Harman helps Cheshire mother of four Helen Broomhead look for a property in the Cinque Terre region of Italy where she can escape from the pressures of day-to-day life

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In the first of five programmes from Liverpool, pensioner Terry White must add to his culinary repertoire when he learns one of his guests is a strict vegan. His scallops have to be extra-special to please fishmonger Dan Redfern - but it is his dessert that leaves the diners lost for words

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In the second Liverpool-based gathering, fishmonger Dan Redfern prepares an ambitious meal that includes more than 10 dishes. Trying to impress wine buff Terry White proves a challenge when the star bottle turns out to be corked, and a conversation about meat distresses vegan Nikki O'Leary

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Nikki O'Leary takes a gamble and prepares a vegan meal for her meat-loving guests in Liverpool. She seems to be on to a winner when fishmonger Dan Redfern asks for third helpings of her aubergine curry, but it remains to be seen whether she has done enough to scoop the £1,000 prize

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Customer service manager Ian Cook tries to impress by catering for his guests' individual needs in Liverpool. He cooks sea bass for fishmonger Dan Redfern, picks the best wines for Terry White and prepares a vegan dish for Nikki O'Leary

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Royalist Margaret Twemlow hosts the final party in Liverpool, and uses quality ingredients as she pulls out all the stops to impress her guests with a venison dish served in castle turrets made of bread, and a selection of desserts. A fireworks display follows the meal, before the winner is revealed

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart is singled out for punishment by school bully Nelson Muntz and seeks advice on self-defence from Grandpa, who joins forces with the owner of the local war memorabilia store to plot the downfall of his grandson's tormentor

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Jazz musician Bleeding Gums Murphy teaches Lisa how to express her gloomy moods through music, and inspires her to write a blues tune for him - which proves unexpectedly popular

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay visits Down City, a restaurant in Providence, Rhode Island. The no-nonsense chef struggles to persuade hot-headed owner Abby to make his suggested changes, and a confrontation results in him being thrown out of the building in the middle of a dinner service. The eatery's co-owner and financier begs Gordon to return, but his success hinges on his ability to win Abby over

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay travels to Fremont, California, in an attempt to save a restaurant that is tearing the owners' marriage apart. Saki Kavouniaris took over Spin a Yarn in 1995 and began to turn it into a success, before marrying bartender Jennifer. When the business started to fail, the couple decided to splash out on refurbishments to recover their glory days, but their efforts have been futile and now their relationship is at stake

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon O'Brien has ambitious plans to turn a pile of old slate into an unusual lamp, while Henry Cole sets his heart on upcycling the seat from an old pony and trap

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Sarah from Wiltshire fell in love with Bulgaria 30 years ago when she was working as a teacher, and now she can't wait to buy a holiday home there. She loves the traditional town of Tryavna's authentic atmosphere and beautiful countryside, and is after a three-bedroom house for her budget of £40,000. Jonnie Irwin shows Sarah and her best friend Suzie five properties to consider

programme.title: Royal Antiques Revived
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell meets three royal repairers as they embark on a journey of incredible restoration. From King George IV's piano to a car that's believed to have belonged to the Queen Mother, and a very rare copy of King Louis XV roll-top desk, every item provides a glimpse into the lives of the people who owned them and the restorers working on them

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts a festive edition of the words and numbers game, with Countdown regulars Susie Dent and Rachel Riley. Jon Richardson and Sarah Millican face off against Sean Lock and David Mitchell, while David O'Doherty joins Susie in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: A look back to the couples taking a second shot at finding love again, including 47-year-old mother of two Emma's date with Peter, and self-confessed geek Sarah, who meet aspiring comic book writer Nick. Having been single for five years following the loss of his partner, Dale had high hopes for 39-year-old massage therapist Chris

programme.title: Christmas at Warwick Castle
programme.desc: Part two of two. In the second programme following life at Warwick Castle during the festive period, 27-year-old historian Melissa Perry helps decorate the castle's historic Daisy sleigh and keeps spirits up with tales of Christmas past, while operations director Liam Bartlett prepares the castle's ice rink, hoping the 93,000 litres of water the local fire brigade pump into the ice rink will freeze in time for the grand December opening

programme.title: Christmas at Highclere Castle
programme.desc: Part one of two. A behind-the-scenes look at life in Highclere Castle - the setting of the popular television series and film Downton Abbey - as they deck their halls and prepare to celebrate Christmas in their own unique way. The programme follows the small team of dedicated staff who are tasked with creating seasonal cheer in the castle, as they race to get everything prepared in time to welcome small groups of guests for guided tours and tea, as well as a virtual gin cocktail party hosted by the Earl and Countess of Carnarvon

programme.title: Luxury Cruises: From Boom to Bust
programme.desc: Kate Quilton examines how ocean travel was transformed from a simple journey to a luxurious holiday in itself. Once the preserve of the wealthy, in the 1980s and 1990s the cruise industry became one of the most extraordinary boom stories, making luxury affordable to the masses. Then Covid shut the industry down. Joined by experts, Kate explores the boom years, the challenges and disasters that rocked the industry, and also considers the post-pandemic future

programme.title: Sarah Beeny's New Life in the Country
programme.desc: Following Sarah Beeny and her family as they relocate from London to Somerset, renovating a semi-derelict former dairy farm into their dream family home. In the sixth episode, Sarah and Graham face new problems as the ground floor of the new home is completely flooded due to a downpour of rain. Later, the family decide they want to adopt some hens from a battery farm, to join the ones they bought from a poultry farm that live a happy, free-range life in their garden

programme.title: Inside John Lewis: Trouble at the Tills
programme.desc: John Lewis has been one of the most iconic names on the British high street for more than a century. But 2020 has been devastating. Steph McGovern investigates challenges facing the company, and what they tell us about the wider future of our high streets as more of us turn to online shopping

programme.title: We're Going on a Bear Hunt
programme.desc: Animated adventure, based on the popular children's bedtime book by Michael Rosen and Helen Oxenbury. Stan, Katie, Rosie, Max and the baby, along with Rufus the dog, all embark on a quest to find some bears. Trekking through whirling snowstorms, muddy bogs and dark forests, the siblings refuse to let anything get in their way. However, when Rosie and Rufus end up getting lost, bear-hunting suddenly doesn't seem quite so much fun after all. Featuring the voices of Olivia Colman, Pam Ferris and Mark Williams as Mum, Grandma and Dad respectively

programme.title: Chef vs Corner Shop
programme.desc: Somerset local Dean Edwards takes on another celebrity chef in the culinary contest, with dishes including Mizo-glazed aubergine, and chicken and artichoke ravioli

programme.title: Junior Bake Off
programme.desc: The young bakers take on two tough challenges set by judges Prue Leith and Liam Charles, including a messy Technical Challenge working with sticky swirls of dough

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin shows five properties to Rachel and Miguel, who have sold their house in Dorset and are now planning a permanent move to Spain's highly sought-after Orihuela Costa

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Summer Sun
programme.desc: Ben Hillman helps house hunters find their dream homes in popular sunny European locations. Potential buyers explore the stunning Croatian island of Hvar

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Children's entertainer and fancy-dress company owner Julia Knight kicks off the competition in Derby. In a bid to win the £1,000 prize, she introduces a Mexican theme, complete with chilli, tortillas, ponchos, sombreros and fake moustaches

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Postman Graham Fox plays host in Derby, hoping his food can reduce the tension following Michele Cooper and Julia Knight's row. The evening is going well until one of the guests finds a piece of crab shell in a fish cake, and by the time the bread and butter pudding is served, the arguing has started again

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Holistic therapist Patsy Sewell-Mwamba rustles up an exotic feast of Caribbean dishes in Derby. The diners dress up to the nines for the occasion, but Julia Knight puts a damper on things by refusing to eat dessert. She is also challenged to try her first oyster - much to the amusement of the other guests

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Erotic-dance teacher Michele Cooper hosts the next party in Derby, hoping her savoury cheesecake, chicken pie and apple tarte Tatin will satisfy her guests' tastebuds. However, the rivalry between her and Julia Knight threatens to ruin the evening, as the picky eater tries to hide food in her handbag

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The final host in Derby is web designer Danny Fletcher, who perplexes his guests by handing them a menu written in French. He also tries to impress with a chicken terrine wrapped in Parma ham, followed by duck a l'orange - but things start to go wrong when he burns the potatoes

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer informs on Krusty the Clown for apparently robbing the Kwik-E-Mart, but Bart is convinced of his hero's innocence and tries to clear his name. With the voice of Kelsey Grammer

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart tries to impress his tough new friends by decapitating a statue of the town's founder Jebediah Springfield, but his act of vandalism only succeeds in turning everybody against him

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads for the Grasshopper Also in Carlstadt, New Jersey, a family-owned Irish restaurant. There, he lends a helping hand to Mitch Sandler, who has been struggling to live up to the expectations of his father-in-law since he invested heavily in the business. The chef also tries to improve the presentation of dishes emerging from the kitchen

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads for Boston to help change the fortunes of a failing Italian restaurant run by two brothers. The eatery's decline began when one of the pair started embezzling money to fund his prescription drug addiction, and things only got worse when the he was sentenced to four years in prison. With the time now served, he has returned, but must first regain his brother's trust before he can save their business

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien search for more items to fix up and sell on, visiting Windsor to meet Pete and Phil, who rent out cars to the film and TV business. Henry finds an Austrian moped which is missing an engine, while Simon picks a fibreglass suit of armour

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jean Johansson helps Glasgow couple Colin and Richard, who have a £200,000 budget to purchase a property in the resort of Playa del Ingles on Gran Canaria

programme.title: Devon and Cornwall
programme.desc: Documentary celebrating the festive season in Devon and Cornwall with people whose livelihood depends on this busy time of year. The first stop is Cornwall's Lizard peninsula, to meet the Richards family, who have been farming this region for more than 500 years. Cameras also follow Devon artist Luna North, whose love of the region inspires her to paint her own Christmas card

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: A chance to catch-up with some of the best moments from this year's episodes, featuring some of the country's favourite celebrities. The line-up critiquing the year's biggest and best shows include Denise van Outen and her partner Eddie, Rylan Clark-Neal and his mum Linda, Gyles Brandreth and Maureen Lipman, Mo Gilligan and Babatunde Aleshe, KSI and S-X, and Shaun Ryder and Bez

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: The comedians gather for the grand final, to find which one of them will be going home with Taskmaster Greg Davies' golden head - a prize that lies within the grasp of four of them. But there are still more games to be played, leading to Katherine Parkinson searching for a spider, Daisy May Cooper destroying a mannequin and Johnny Vegas's pocket watch finally coming in handy

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Fred Sirieix and his team head for the British countryside to celebrate love in the most festive of locations, where 28-year-old Holly goes on a date with 32-year-old Shane. Elsewhere, Paul, who has never had a boyfriend, is set up with 35-year-old IT worker Alex from London, and Shauna and James, who are both from Dewsbury, enjoy a festive date

programme.title: The Crystal Maze
programme.desc: Richard Ayoade guides families through the four zones of the Maze, tackling a range of skill, mystery, physical and mental challenges

programme.title: Christmas at Warwick Castle
programme.desc: Part two of two. In the second programme following life at Warwick Castle during the festive period, 27-year-old historian Melissa Perry helps decorate the castle's historic Daisy sleigh and keeps spirits up with tales of Christmas past, while operations director Liam Bartlett prepares the castle's ice rink, hoping the 93,000 litres of water the local fire brigade pump into the ice rink will freeze in time for the grand December opening

programme.title: Royal Antiques Revived
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell meets three royal repairers as they embark on a journey of incredible restoration. From King George IV's piano to a car that's believed to have belonged to the Queen Mother, and a very rare copy of King Louis XV roll-top desk, every item provides a glimpse into the lives of the people who owned them and the restorers working on them

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: George is joined by designer, craftsman and maker Will Hardie on trips to Norway, Finland, the Alps and Canada to check out some amazing winter designs - from ice hotels to stunning modern homes. Along the way, the duo build an igloo, race toboggans, feed reindeer and dodge a bear

programme.title: Jamie: Keep Cooking at Christmas
programme.desc: Part one of two. Jamie Oliver looks at ways to make gifts to share with loved ones, including roasted mulled wine ham that's cooked and glazed in all the flavours of Christmas. Accompanied by cheeses you can flavour and deliver ready to be baked. Jamie and the children also create the ultimate yule log for their grandparents

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.desc: Showbiz news and gossip

programme.title: It's Your Fault I'm Fat
programme.desc: Sixteen-year-old Ellouise from Newbury weighs in at just over fourteen and a half stone. Together with mum Sarah, she is determined to fight to shed some pounds

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Charlie is nominated for an advertising award but decides to boycott the ceremony, having lost to a rival several years in a row. The rest of the family try to con him into going. Comedy, starring Charlie Sheen

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Alan encounters financial problems when he starts paying for all his girlfriend's bills, and gets into trouble with Judith for not paying his alimony cheque on time. Comedy, starring Jon Cryer and Charlie Sheen

programme.title: The Nightmare Neighbour Next Door
programme.desc: Wiltshire couple Edward Lea and his wife found their happy retirement was short-lived when an imposing 8ft breeze-block wall - dubbed the Great Wall of China - was built by their neighbour between their properties. In Scotland, Karen Ryrie and her elderly mother are fed up with living opposite John Tyer, his three dogs and a huge family of rabbits. Following their complaint about the smell, Karen's subsequent accusation of assault has made the dispute far more serious. Elsewhere, Linda Bird had not been expecting to wear headphones around the house when she moved to Bedfordshire for a quiet life, but the issue has escalated beyond excessive noise to become a battle over boundaries with neighbour Stanislav Fajdel

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: The officers chase a debt of almost £2,000 in unpaid parking fines, and later encounter the extraordinary case of a debt of £80,000 owed by a hotel owner to his ex-girlfriend. However, as they begin to search the property, they call upon police back-up as the agents fear the debtor may have access to firearms. Elsewhere, two officers head to a car garage in Manchester where the owner owes £2,000 after losing a court case over a faulty vehicle

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Agents chase more than £1,700 in unpaid parking fines in south-east London, but struggle to pinpoint the debtor. Meanwhile, officers in north Wales try to recover the £2,000 owed by a tradesman to a former employer, and in Kent agents chase £8,000 owed by a jewellery shop to two former employees

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: The agents try to recover more than £12,000 owed by a jeweller to one of his suppliers, but as the tension mounts, the debtor violently lashes out at one of the officers. Meanwhile, agents in London chase up more than £5,000 owed by a tenant to her former landlord for rent arrears, and also attempt to retrieve over £6,000 owed by a property developer

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: The officers try to recover £13,000 owed by a high-performance car dealer in Birmingham, and in Wolverhampton the agents chase nearly £2,000 owed to a car hire company. Elsewhere, officers visit a family in Kent to try to collect more than £5,000, but an emotional backstory means they are forced to make a sensitive decision

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The Operational Support Unit has to evade a guard dog when carrying out a drugs raid on a farm, while a driver thinks he can pull a fast one by impersonating his brother. Barry finds himself dealing with an irate nightclubber, Craggy has an early morning encounter with a polecat and it is a case of deja vu for Dan as he deals with the same person twice in one shift

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Sgt Jon Moon and his police dog Kanto follow a trail of broken glass and spilt alcohol, leading to a confrontation with a robber wielding a nasty-looking knife. Elsewhere, a fight in a club ends with one man in handcuffs and another in hospital, Barry Peach plays catch-up as a teenage driver tears up the streets of Skegness, and Jon Peacock detects a whiff of cannabis in a quiet rural village

programme.title: Nativity 3: Dude, Where's My Donkey?! (2014)
programme.desc: The pupils of St Bernadette's face a new challenge as Christmas approaches, when their new teacher loses his memory, forgetting everything about the festive season and that he is about to get married. It falls to the kids, led by excitable teacher Mr Poppy, to help him remember both in time for the wedding. Comedy sequel, starring Martin Clunes, Catherine Tate, Marc Wootton and Celia Imrie

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: The agents try to recover more than £12,000 owed by a jeweller to one of his suppliers, but as the tension mounts, the debtor violently lashes out at one of the officers. programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: The officers try to recover £13,000 owed by a high-performance car dealer in Birmingham, and in Wolverhampton the agents chase nearly £2,000 owed to a car hire company. programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Officers chase £4,500 in court fees in north Wales after a property deal turned sour, and in Birmingham the agents try to collect over £3,500 from a single mum who defaulted on loan repayments. Elsewhere in Worcestershire, the enforcement agents try to recover more than £10,000 from a shop-keeper for fraudulently keeping a deposit during a property sale

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Members of the operational support unit of Lincolnshire's Roads Policing Unit smash through a bedroom window in a drugs raid, a speeding driver is clocked at over 140mph, and friendships are pushed to the limit when amphetamines are found in a car. Plus, vehicle thieves try - and fail - to outpace the interceptors, and an officer gets help from a very unexpected source

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Officers step in to deal with a rowdy reveller when things get out of hand on New Year's Eve in Lincoln city centre, while Suzie Ranyard catches out a motorist who claims he has been driving with a dodgy number plate for 18 years. Jon Moon arrests a shoplifter more than happy to admit to his crime and a young lad discovers how hard it is to pull the wool over the eyes of Jon Peacock

programme.title: Inside
programme.desc: A behind-the-scenes look at the inner workings of the supermarket during the festive period, revealing the secrets of the retailer's success, and how it rose from a grocery chain that once baffled its UK customers, to a brand that took almost one billion pounds in December 2018, enjoying its busiest week to date. The programme also follows the retailer from its arrival in Britain in the 1990s, through changing times and changing tastes to become the brand it is today, and reveals how the discounter came to fill television screens at Christmas, taking on the big name brand adverts with their own festive versions

programme.title: Secrets of the Mega Christmas Buffet
programme.desc: In August, Channel 5 broadcast a documentary about the JRC Global Buffet, which can serve as many as 3,000 people every day. Now cameras visit the Wembley branch to see how staff cope at the busiest time of the year. Christmas dinner with all the trimmings is on the menu in December, meaning the chefs cook an amazing 3,000kg of turkey as well as thousands of sausages and sprouts. But they also serve up festive variations on their traditional Asian treats too, going all out to make the experience as fun as possible for their customers

programme.title: Bargain-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: Seasonal special of the documentary following expats on the Costa del Sol. Novice shopkeepers Russell and Lindsay have a crisis on their hands when the meat-and-veg packs they have ordered are delivered in the wrong sizes. Ex-nurse Susie and husband Reg spend Christmas Day volunteering at a dog shelter in Estepona, and Doncaster lass Margaret belts out a few tunes with the choir - but they are a bit out of tune. Scottish couple Ivor and Barbara prepare for their first Christmas in Spain since arriving six months ago

programme.title: Queen - Live at Wembley

programme.title: Fred Dinenage: Murder Casebook

programme.desc: Ten years ago, in November 2006, Al Jazeera English was launched. To mark that anniversary, we've created a new show, REWIND, which over the coming weeks will let us re-screen and update some of the channel's most memorable and award-winning documentaries of the past decade. Filmed against the dramatic backdrop of Doha's iconic Museum of Islamic Art, our team will be finding out what happened to some of the characters in those programmes and asking how their stories have changed in the years since our cameras left

programme.title: Britain's Greatest Generation
programme.desc: Stories of courage, suffering and survival from servicemen and women between 1942 and 1945, as revealed by a generation of people now in their nineties and hundreds. This instalment includes the story of Fergus Anckorn who was once the youngest member of the Magic Circle and - at 96 - is now the oldest. He was taken prisoner by the Japanese in Singapore and put to work on the Burma Railway. He managed to survive using his magic to entertain his captors, who rewarded him with food that kept him and his fellow prisoners alive

programme.title: Filthy Cities
programme.desc: Dan Snow experiences what New York was like at the height of the Industrial Revolution. Millions fled to escape the persecution, poverty and famine of 19th-century Europe, only to be confronted by living conditions worse than those they had left behind. He explores the overcrowded slums of Lower Manhattan, which were a breeding ground for cholera and typhoid, and has a go at cooking using clothes dye and floor cleaner - ingredients that were added to meals to disguise the smell of spoiled meat. He also discovers how certain feats of engineering transformed not only the city, but the world

programme.title: The Exception (2016)

Drama, starring Lily James, Jai Courtney and Christopher Plummer

programme.title: The Legend of Barney Thomson (2015)
programme.desc: A mild-mannered Glasgow barber finds his mundane life turned upside down when he accidentally commits murder. Soon one killing leads to another, and his desperate efforts to cover up his crimes leads him to uncover his mother's dark secrets. Black comedy based on the series of books by Douglas Lindsay, directed by and starring Robert Carlyle, with Emma Thompson and Ray Winstone

programme.desc: A French woman living in London ends her relationship with her boyfriend when she decides to return to her own country. programme.title: World Darts Championship
programme.desc: The afternoon session on day nine of this year's tournament. This stage last year saw Fallon Sherrock shock the darts world with a 3-1 win over Austrian Mensur Suljovic in the second round. That win was Sherrock's second victory in the tournament, after her history-making win over Ted Evetts. Other winners at this stage last year were Australia's Simon Whitlock, and Englishmen Adrian Lewis, Daryl Gurney and Glen Durrant

programme.title: Test Cricket
programme.desc: England v Sri Lanka. Highlights of the opening day of the First Test in the two-match series, which takes place at Lord's. When these sides faced each other in a Test match here in 2011 it finished in a draw, with both teams posting a first innings total in excess of 450

programme.title: Test Cricket
programme.desc: England v Sri Lanka. Highlights of the second day of the First Test in the two-match series, which takes place at Lord's. Five of England's batsmen recorded first-innings scores of over 50 when the sides met here in 2011, with Matt Prior's 126 the standout performance, especially with him coming to the crease with the score on 201-5

programme.title: The Ashes
programme.desc: England v Australia. Highlights of the opening day of the First Ashes Test at Sophia Gardens in Cardiff, as the great rivals met once more to decide the winner of the famous urn. The only previous occasion the teams played on this ground in a Test match was 2009 - also the first game of that series - when the home side snatched a draw from the jaws of defeat on the fifth day, Paul Collingwood digging in for a typically gritty 74 and the final pair of James Anderson and Monty Panesar holding out against the Aussie bowling attack for 40 agonising minutes

programme.title: The Ashes
programme.desc: England v Australia. Highlights of the opening day's play in the Fourth Ashes Test, which took place at Trent Bridge in Nottingham. This venue was the setting for the First Test back in 2013, and day one saw a total of 14 wickets fall - England were bowled out for 215 in their first innings and the tourists ended the day on 75/4 in reply. Peter Siddle was the pick of the Australian bowlers, taking 5/50 in 14 overs. No English batsman passed 50, with Jonathan Trott coming closest on 48 before being bowled by Siddle

programme.title: The Ashes
programme.desc: England v Australia. Highlights of the second day's play in the Fourth Ashes Test at Trent Bridge. The home side won a closely fought contest the last time the teams met here in 2013, and day two was an especially dramatic one. Teenage spinner Ashton Agar - making his Test debut - came to the crease as the last Australian batsman, with the tourists in desperate trouble at 117/9 in their first innings. However, Agar and Phillip Hughes went on to record the highest 10th-wicket partnership in Test history, scoring 163 runs between them before the youngster was caught for 98 off the bowling of Stuart Broad, falling agonisingly short of what would have been a remarkable century

programme.title: Test Cricket
programme.desc: England v Pakistan. Action from the opening day of the Second Test in the four-match 2016 series, which took place at Old Trafford

programme.title: Test Cricket
programme.desc: England v Pakistan. Action from day two of the Second Test in the four-match 2016 series, which took place at Old Trafford

programme.title: ICC World T20 Cricket
programme.desc: South Africa v England. Action from the Group 1 contest, which came from the Wankhede Stadium in Mumbai

programme.title: Test Cricket
programme.desc: England v South Africa. Highlights of the opening day's play in the 2017 First Test, which took place at Lord's

programme.title: The Ashes
programme.desc: England v Australia. Action from the second day of the Second Test at Lord's, where the five-match series continued. Stuart Broad recorded a career-best 7-44 in a man-of-the-match second-innings performance against New Zealand here two months ago, while James Anderson became only the fourth Englishman to surpass 300 Test wickets when taking 5-47 in the first innings

programme.title: The Ashes
programme.desc: England v Australia. Action from the third day of the Second Test at Lord's, where the five-match series continued. Then-England captain Andrew Strauss produced a fine first-innings knock of 161 when the sides last met here in this format in 2009 and the hosts went on to win by 115 runs, a victory that meant they finally ended their long winless run in Tests against the Aussies at this venue that spanned all the way back to 1934

programme.title: ICC Cricket World Cup
programme.desc: England v West Indies. Action from the group-stage match, which took place at The Hampshire Bowl in Southampton

programme.desc: England v South Africa. Highlights of the opening day's play in the 2017 First Test, which took place at Lord's

programme.title: The Ashes
programme.desc: England v Australia. Action from day five of the First Test at Trent Bridge, where the opening contest of the five-match series was brought to a conclusion. Having decided to replace their head coach Mickey Arthur with former batsman Darren Lehmann just 16 days prior to the commencement of this series, Australia will have hoped to claim an unlikely victory in this encounter that could spur them on in the coming Test matches

programme.title: Test Cricket
programme.desc: England v India. Highlights of the opening day of the Fourth Test at Old Trafford as the five-match series continued. The series is level at 1-1 after England recorded a 266-run victory at the Ageas Bowl last time out, which was their first Test victory since August 2013

programme.title: Test Cricket
programme.desc: England v Sri Lanka. Highlights of the third day of the First Test at Headingley, where the three-match series continued

programme.title: Test Cricket
programme.desc: England v India

programme.title: The Ashes
programme.desc: England v Australia. Highlights from the Emirates Durham International Cricket Ground, where the five-match series continued with the fourth day of the Fourth Test. The touring Australians have been beset by problems on and off the pitch since they arrived in this country, beginning with the ICC Champions Trophy which led into this Test series, but put in a much-improved performance in the Third Test. England have already retained the Ashes following the draw at Old Trafford and can afford to concentrate on producing their best cricket, as they aim to outline their superiority over Australia as the nations play back-to-back Test series to avoid a clash with the 2015 World Cup, which will be held in Australia and New Zealand
